name: CruiseApp
description: A mobile-first cruise companion for itinerary planning, trip discovery and on-board information.
overview: CruiseApp brings voyage planning, destinations and practical travel information into one focused experience.
problem: Cruise information is fragmented across booking sites, email and operator portals, making trip planning unnecessarily difficult.
solution: A fast cross-platform application that centralizes itineraries and travel context with real-time cloud data.
architecture: Flutter clients communicate with Firebase Authentication, Firestore and serverless functions through a typed service layer.
businessImpact: Reduces the time travelers spend assembling trip information and creates a reusable digital channel for voyage services.
challenges: Designing resilient offline behavior while keeping itinerary data synchronized across devices.
lessonsLearned: Mobile travel products benefit from progressive disclosure and a strong offline-first data model.

name: LR Trading
description: A trading analytics platform combining market data, portfolio signals and risk-aware decision support.
overview: LR Trading is an analytics workspace built to turn high-volume market data into clear, actionable signals.
problem: Useful signals are often spread across slow, disconnected tools with limited portfolio context.
solution: A unified dashboard with automated ingestion, analytics services and configurable risk indicators.
architecture: A React interface consumes Python analytics APIs backed by scheduled data pipelines and containerized cloud services.
businessImpact: Consolidates research workflows and shortens the path from market movement to informed action.
challenges: Normalizing inconsistent market feeds and communicating uncertainty without adding visual noise.
lessonsLearned: Reliable decision tools expose data quality and confidence alongside every recommendation.

name: Libada
description: A SaaS operations platform that streamlines service workflows, scheduling and customer communication.
overview: Libada gives small service teams one place to coordinate work and customer activity.
problem: Manual handoffs across spreadsheets, messages and calendars create missed work and poor visibility.
solution: A multi-tenant workflow platform with automation at the points where teams commonly lose time.
architecture: A TypeScript single-page app uses Firebase services with isolated tenant data and event-driven automation.
businessImpact: Replaces fragmented administration with a repeatable workflow and clearer operational ownership.
challenges: Keeping flexible workflows simple enough for non-technical users.
lessonsLearned: Good SaaS defaults matter more than endless configuration options.

name: Anonymous Chat
description: A privacy-first real-time chat experience with ephemeral rooms and minimal identity footprint.
overview: Anonymous Chat enables low-friction conversations without account creation or persistent social profiles.
problem: Many chat tools require identity, installation or lengthy onboarding for a short-lived conversation.
solution: Shareable ephemeral rooms with real-time messaging and deliberate data minimization.
architecture: A React client subscribes to scoped Firebase channels protected by strict lifecycle and access rules.
businessImpact: Removes onboarding friction for temporary support, community and event conversations.
challenges: Balancing anonymity with abuse prevention and responsible data retention.
lessonsLearned: Privacy features must be encoded in the data lifecycle, not only described in the interface.

name: MegaBoost
description: An automation platform for building, executing and measuring repeatable growth workflows.
overview: MegaBoost transforms recurring campaign operations into observable, repeatable automations.
problem: Growth teams lose time moving data and coordinating repetitive actions across disconnected systems.
solution: A visual control plane backed by scheduled workers, reusable workflow definitions and measurable outcomes.
architecture: TypeScript control plane, Python worker services, durable queues and containerized deployment on Azure.
businessImpact: Creates operational leverage by turning manual campaign playbooks into reliable systems.
challenges: Making third-party integrations resilient to rate limits and changing APIs.
lessonsLearned: Automation products need first-class observability and safe replay from day one.

name: AI Engineering Drawing Recognition
description: A computer vision pipeline that extracts components, dimensions and metadata from engineering drawings.
overview: An AI document pipeline for turning complex technical drawings into validated structured information.
problem: Manual review of large drawing sets is slow, specialized and vulnerable to missed details.
solution: A hybrid vision and language pipeline detects drawing regions, extracts attributes and validates results against engineering rules.
architecture: Azure-hosted ingestion triggers Python vision workers, multimodal extraction and a human validation queue.
businessImpact: Accelerates document intake and makes engineering information searchable and reusable downstream.
challenges: Handling inconsistent drawing conventions, scan quality and domain-specific notation.
lessonsLearned: Specialized AI succeeds when probabilistic extraction is paired with deterministic validation.

name: AI Contract Review Assistant
description: An AI-assisted review system that identifies clauses, risks and deviations from preferred contract positions.
overview: A secure review workspace that helps teams understand contracts faster while keeping experts in control.
problem: Initial contract review is repetitive, time-consuming and difficult to standardize across teams.
solution: Retrieval-grounded analysis maps clauses to a review playbook, highlights evidence and produces actionable findings.
architecture: Encrypted document ingestion feeds retrieval and structured LLM workflows, with citations stored alongside every finding.
businessImpact: Reduces first-pass review effort and creates a consistent, auditable review process.
challenges: Controlling hallucinations and maintaining traceability for high-stakes recommendations.
lessonsLearned: Trust comes from evidence, clear uncertainty and deliberate human approval gates.

name: Jira AI Risk Analyzer
tagline: See delivery risk before it compounds.
description: An AI service that analyzes Jira delivery signals to surface project risks and recommended interventions.
overview: A delivery intelligence layer that turns project activity into timely, explainable risk signals.
problem: Delivery risks often remain hidden in issue history until deadlines or dependencies are already affected.
solution: Automated analysis combines workflow metrics with contextual AI summaries and evidence-backed recommendations.
architecture: Jira webhooks and scheduled sync feed an Azure-hosted Python analysis service and a secure reporting dashboard.
businessImpact: Helps delivery leaders intervene earlier and focus attention on the work most likely to affect outcomes.
challenges: Distinguishing genuine risk from normal workflow variation across different team practices.
lessonsLearned: Operational AI must adapt to team context and show the evidence behind each signal.
